content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes the radius of a circle as input and prints its area. (use π\piπ = 3.14)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer R, the radius of a circle.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the area of the circle on its own line, rounded to one decimal place.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case with radius 5. Area = 3.14 * 5 * 5 = 78.5, printed with one decimal.
One test case with radius 10. Area = 3.14 * 10 * 10 = 314.0.
One test case with radius 1. Area = 3.14 * 1 * 1 = 3.14, rounded to one decimal gives 3.1.

solution.approach: For each test case read the radius R, compute area = 3.14 * R * R and print the result with exactly one digit after the decimal point. The formula is O(1) per test case, so the whole algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time computation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().splitlines()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
for i in range(1, T + 1):
    R = float(data[i].strip())
    area = 3.14 * R * R
    out.append(f"{area:.1f}")
print('\n'.join(out))
